params.lat: number — user latitude in decimal degrees
params.lon: number — user longitude in decimal degrees
params.radiusMeters: number — search radius for the Overpass around filter

shape.stores: array of store records (empty when status is 'empty' or 'error'); each record: { name: string, address: string, lat: number, lon: number }
shape.message: string — human-readable detail, populated when status is 'empty' or 'error' (e.g. network failure, timeout, no stores in radius)

notes: section-2 (data lookup) returns normalized store records; it does NOT compute distances, rank, or select a closest store — that is section-3's job. Each store record always carries a name, an address string, and numeric coordinates; if OSM data lacks a name or address, section-2 substitutes a clear placeholder string ('Unnamed liquor store' / 'Address not listed') rather than omitting the field, so section-3 can render without null checks. status communicates network/empty conditions so section-3 can render the correct empty/error state. This contract passes data, not logic.
